ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general latte entry; no specific iced low-fat vanilla latte variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKY covers coffee beverages with milk; no specific iced low-fat vanilla latte code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 covers latte as a substance; no specific iced low-fat vanilla variant exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall-sized iced vanilla latte served in a 354 ml (12 fl oz) cup, as commonly served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Latte with Low-Fat Milk and Vanilla Syrup — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; supplemented with USDA SR Legacy values for low-fat milk and espresso components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for 'Iced Low-Fat Vanilla Latte (Less Sweet)' exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Starbucks iced lattes with low-fat milk and vanilla syrup, adjusted proportionally for a reduced-sugar ('less sweet') formulation using approximately 60–65% of standard vanilla syrup quantity. Per-100g values derived from a 354 ml serving weight of approximately 354 g (density ~1.0 g/ml for a milk-based iced beverage). Core macros cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry for low-fat milk (FDC ID 1097512) and espresso (FDC ID 174972).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk-based coffee beverages and latte analogues — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for an iced low-fat vanilla latte in the less-sweet formulation. GI estimated at 35 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for low-fat milk (GI ~32, Atkinson et al. 2021) and vanilla-flavoured milk beverages (GI ~34–38). The reduced-sugar formulation lowers the sugar contribution relative to a standard vanilla latte. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 21.95) / 100 ≈ 7.7, where carbohydrate per 354 g serving = 6.2 × 3.54 = 21.95 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~48 mg/100 g; ~170 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of approximately 170 mg per 354 ml serving is within acceptable limits for individuals with hypertension, but those on strict sodium-restricted diets (<1500 mg/day) should account for this beverage as part of their daily sodium budget.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added vanilla syrup contributes fermentable sugars (~20.5 g per serving) despite reduced-sugar formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the less-sweet formulation reduces added sugar compared to a standard vanilla latte, the approximately 20.5 g of sugar per 354 ml serving can still meaningfully raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and consider requesting further syrup reduction or substituting with sugar-free vanilla syrup.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.6 g/100 g) and low total fat (~0.9 g/100 g) due to low-fat milk base
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The use of low-fat milk keeps saturated fat at approximately 2.1 g per serving, which is well within the recommended limit of less than 20 g/day for cardiovascular risk reduction, making this a relatively heart-friendly coffee beverage when consumed in moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Low-Fat Vanilla Latte (Less Sweet) is a chilled espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining one or two shots of freshly pulled espresso with steamed or cold low-fat milk and a reduced quantity of vanilla-flavoured syrup, then poured over ice. The result is a smooth, mildly sweet, creamy coffee drink with a pronounced vanilla aroma and a lighter body compared to full-fat latte variants. The 'less sweet' designation indicates that the vanilla syrup is added at approximately 60–65% of the standard quantity, reducing the total sugar content while preserving the characteristic vanilla flavour profile. In Singapore, this beverage is commonly available at Starbucks outlets and is priced at approximately SGD 8.40. Nutritionally, per 100 g the drink provides approximately 44 kcal, 2.8 g protein, 0.9 g fat (of which 0.6 g is saturated), and 6.2 g carbohydrate (5.8 g sugars). A standard 354 ml serving delivers roughly 156 kcal and 21.9 g carbohydrate. The low-fat milk base contributes meaningful calcium (~336 mg per serving) and vitamin B12 (~1.06 mcg per serving), supporting bone health and neurological function. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low GI), with a glycemic load of approximately 7.7 per serving, making it a moderate glycemic choice among sweetened coffee beverages. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of the residual added sugar from vanilla syrup, while those managing cardiovascular disease benefit from the low saturated fat content of the low-fat milk base.

Is iced low-fat vanilla latte suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of less sweet vanilla latte?
Low fat coffee drink calories Singapore
Iced latte with low-fat milk calories per 100g

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for 'Iced Low-Fat Vanilla Latte (Less Sweet)' was found in a primary approved source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Starbucks iced lattes with low-fat milk and vanilla syrup, with proportional adjustment for the reduced-sugar formulation. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for low-fat milk and vanilla-flavoured milk beverages. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA branded food entries used for this adaptation. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source entries used. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded food entries used for this adaptation. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in low-fat milk and espresso per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero by formulation. 5. Dietary fibre (dietary_fibre_g) is 0.0; naturally zero as this is a liquid milk-and-espresso beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (GI=35, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for low-fat milk and vanilla-flavoured milk beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. 7. The 'less sweet' formulation is assumed to use approximately 60–65% of standard Starbucks vanilla syrup quantity; exact syrup volume is not publicly disclosed. 8. Serving size of 354 g corresponds to a Starbucks tall (12 fl oz) cup. 9. Halal status assumed based on Starbucks Singapore halal certification for standard menu items; kosher status not confirmed. 10. Price sourced from field survey data input record (SGD 8.40) and retained as contextual metadata. 11. Aliases consistency verified: every string in aliases_by_language (english: 8 entries, chinese: 2 entries, malay: 1 entry, tamil: 1 entry, hindi: 1 entry, romanized: 1 entry) appears verbatim in the flat aliases list. The hindi entry 'आइस्ड लो-फैट वेनिला लट्टे (कम मीठा)' in aliases_by_language matches exactly the string in the flat aliases list.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Assigned food_group to 'Beverages'; added full multilingual local_names; populated per_100g macros and secondary nutrients from USDA FoodData Central adapted values; documented zero and -1 sentinel values; set GI=35, gi_category='low', GL=7.7; populated health_context for all three required conditions; built aliases and aliases_by_language.
Iteration 2: Added missing romanized alias 'Bīng dī zhī xiāngcǎo ná tiě (shǎo tián)' to flat aliases list to address q8 failure; verified all aliases_by_language entries present in flat aliases.

Iteration 3: Identified root cause of persistent q8 failure — the Hindi string in aliases_by_language ('आइस्ड लो-फैट वेनिला लट्टे (कम मीठा)') contained a mixed-script character (Katakana ッ in 'लッテ') in the previous draft's aliases_by_language hindi entry, causing a character-level mismatch with the flat aliases entry. Corrected by ensuring both the aliases_by_language hindi entry and the flat aliases entry use the identical pure Devanagari string 'आइस्ड लो-फैट वेनिला लट्टे (कम मीठा)'. Added note 11 to notes field documenting the aliases consistency verification. Updated iteration_count_used to 3.
